titleStmt.title: SII 31.186: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 186. (A.R. No. B 354 of 1937.) SIDDHĀNTAM, GUDIVADA TALUK, KRISHNA DISTRICT.

summary.p: Pillar lying in front of the Nāgēśvarasvāmi temple.
This is dated in Śaka 1326, Tāraṇa, Māgha, śu. 10, Tuesday corresponding to 22 January, 1405 A.D.

It states that the pillars were caused to be made of the mukhāmaṇḍapa of the temple of Nagarēsvaradēva at Koṁḍavīḍu by Rāvipūṁḍi Vīriseṭṭi of Sidhiseṭlagōtra for the merit of his father Saraṇiseṭṭi and mother Mārasāni. The donor is described as the lord of Penugoṇḍapura and Vaiśyakulōttama.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . śubha śakava¨rṣaṁbulu 1326 Agu
nēṁṭi tāraṇa saṁvvatsara māṣu śu 10 ma .
koṁḍavīṭi nagarēśvaradēvara mukhamaṁḍa
pānaku nagarēśvaradēvadivyaśrī pādapadmā
rādhakuṁḍḍunu penuṁgoṁḍa puravarādhī
śvaruṁḍunu vaiśyakulōttamuṁḍunu sithi
seṭlagōtra pavitruṁḍununaina rāvipūṁḍi
viriseṭṭi tamataṁḍri saraṇaseṭṭekiṁni ta
ma talli mārasānikiṁni puṇyamugānuce
Iṁccina kaṁbamulu . maṁgaḷamahāśrī
śrī śrīṁ jēyunu .
Digital edition of SII 31.186 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
